mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or stays pinned near zero, discount rates on equities remain compressed and risk appetite is supported, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered by low or declining FEDFUNDS, 20-day SPX returns are negative in most instances or the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a recent local threshold signals worsening household income and labor demand, which reduces expected sales and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE threshold crossings are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if the sign of the effect disappears after controlling for recent market trend.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS relative to prior months indicates stronger current consumer spending and revenue growth expectations, which supports equity prices and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not correspond to positive 5-day SPX returns, or if the sign is negative in a rolling 3-year sample despite continued sales growth.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO and PAYEMS both weaken at the same time, firms face lower output and softer hiring, which is a genuine growth scare that should push S&P 500 returns down over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in INDPRO and PAYEMS fail to predict negative 20-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the effect fully without interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A disinflation shock, identified by CPIAUCSL growth slowing while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged or rising, lowers expected policy pressure and improves valuation multiples, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation trend falls below its 6-month average while FEDFUNDS is unchanged or higher
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation plus steady or higher policy rates do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the pattern reverses during similar historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in HOUST signals weaker housing demand and a slower downstream spending cycle, so S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days should be negative.
failure_condition: Reject if sustained HOUST weakness is not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns or if the relationship is indistinguishable from random in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a rise in UNRATE combined with flat or falling PAYEMS marks a labor-market deterioration that should amplify recession fears and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the labor deterioration pair does not outperform either series alone in predicting negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect vanishes outside tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: If GDPC1 growth decelerates while RSAFS remains weak, the economy is entering a demand slowdown that compresses earnings expectations and should lower S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent weakness in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not predict negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the signal becomes positive in repeated late-cycle samples.
